revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|Thumbnail]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Bungotakada|ja|豊後高田市}}, Ōita Prefecture. It holds the rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|ja|防府天満宮}} in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|粟嶋社}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|ja|宇佐駅}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

{{Shrine |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|Thumbnail]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Bungotakada|ja|豊後高田市}}, Ōita Prefecture. It holds the rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|ja|防府天満宮}} in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|粟嶋社}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|ja|宇佐駅}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

revision.text:
{{Shrine |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|Thumbnail]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Bungotakada|ja|豊後高田市}}, Ōita Prefecture. It holds the rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命}} * {{ill|Ichikishimahime|ja|市寸嶋比売命}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|ja|防府天満宮}} in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造}} design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|粟嶋社}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|ja|宇佐駅}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|サムネイル]] |所在地 = 大分県豊後高田市高田671 |位置 = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 大鷦鷯尊 |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = 伝仁寿2年（852年） |本殿 = 三間社流造檜皮葺 |別名 = 若宮様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 陰暦10月15日に近い金曜日から日曜日 |神事 = 神幸祭・川渡し神事（例祭日に同じ）
ホーランエンヤ（1月上旬の日曜日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=若宮八幡神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''若宮八幡神社'''（わかみやはちまんじんじゃ）は[[大分県]][[豊後高田市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]。通称'''若宮様'''（わかみやさま）。 正月に行われる[[ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|ホーランエンヤ]]と11月（陰暦10月）に行われる秋季大祭の'''川渡し神事'''（裸祭り）の2つの伝統行事で有名。また、[[紫陽花]]の名所としても有名である。 == 祭神 == '''大鷦鷯尊'''（おほさざきのみこと。[[仁徳天皇]]）を主祭神に、[[天照大御神]]、[[玉祖命]]、'''品陀別命'''（ほむたわけのみこと、[[応神天皇]]）、[[隼総別皇子]]、[[大葉枝皇子]]、[[小葉枝皇子]]、[[雌鳥皇女]]、[[多岐都比売命]]、[[多紀理毘売命]]、[[市寸嶋比売命]]、[[天忍穂耳尊|天之忍穗耳命]]、[[天之菩卑能命]]、[[天津日子根命]]、[[活津日子根命]]、[[熊野久須毘命|熊野久須比命]]（多岐都比売命以下は所謂[[五男三女神]]）を配祀する。 == 由緒沿革 == [[国東半島]]の6郷のうちの[[来縄郷|来縄（くなわ）郷]]他は[[武蔵郷|武蔵（むさし）]]、[[国東郷|国東]]、[[田染郷|田染（たしぶ）]]、[[安岐郷|安岐（あき）]]、[[伊美郷|伊美（いみ）]]の5郷。における[[宇佐神宮]]の[[別宮]]で、[[天長]]元年（[[824年]]）に[[豊前国]][[宇佐郡]]の住人、[[大神蘊麻呂]]の母の酒井門主女に[[神託]]が下り、それを受けて[[仁寿]]2年（[[852年]]）の12月に創祀されたと伝えられる。なお、天照大神、玉祖神、品陀別命の3柱は、[[養老]]2年（[[718年]]）に豊後高田市内の字御玉に出現したという玉を神体として祀られていたという。 [[元亀]]2年（[[1571年]]）と[[天正]]7年（[[1579年]]）の二度にわたり[[キリシタン大名]]である[[大友義鎮|大友宗麟]]らによって焼き討ちされ、天正度においては[[大友氏]]に背いた[[田原親貫]]に味方したとして[[大友義統]]から[[社領]]を没収されたが、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[高田城 (豊後国)|高田城]]城主に封じられた[[竹中重利]]により文禄年中に再興された。その後高田を領した[[島原藩]]藩主[[松平忠房 (島原藩主)|松平忠房]]により[[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）に高田の宗祠と定められ、以来歴代藩主の尊崇を受け、[[天保]]4年（[[1833年]]）には藩主[[松平忠侯]]によって社殿が造替された。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に[[郷社]]に列し、同23年6月に県社に昇格した。なお、[[昭和]]30年（[[1955年]]）に[[桂川 (大分県)|桂川]]の改修のために約80[[メートル]]東方に移転している。 == 祭祀 == === 秋季大祭 === 陰暦10月中旬に豊作を感謝する秋季大祭（例祭）が斎行される。例祭にともない[[神幸祭]]と[[神輿]]が桂川を渡る「川渡し神事」が行われ、これは[[永保]]4年（[[1084年]]）に荒行のひとつとして始められたと伝えられるが、神輿の舁き手の姿から「裸祭り」とも呼ばれている。かつては陰暦10月14日から16日にかけて行われていたが、現在は陰暦10月15日に近い金曜日から日曜日にかけて斎行されている。 祭りの一週間前に当神社から神幸先の宮町二区の[[お旅所]]（下宮）までの要所要所に注連を立て、金曜日午後から神輿が当神社を進発する（お下り）。神輿の舁き手には陸組と川組とがあるが、まず頭に[[鉢巻]]を締めた白装束の男子数十人からなる陸組に担がれて豊後高田市の市役所を練った後、夕刻に御玉橋の右岸袂に建つ大鳥居で川組に交代、川組は頭に鉢巻を下半身に[[褌]]と[[腹帯 (曖昧さ回避)|腹帯]]を締めて白衣を着した[[草鞋]]履きの男子数十人からなり、神輿を担いで桂川に入り、浮き沈みしながらも掛け声を上げ、数隻のお供船に導かれつつ川を渡る。対岸に着くと再度陸組に代わり、町を練って下宮に入る。その後神輿は下宮に駐輦し、日曜日午後に下宮を進発（お上り）、再び桂川を渡る「川渡し神事」を経て当神社に還幸する染矢多喜男「大分の祭礼行事・解説 裸祭り」（『祭礼行事・大分県』所収）。。 この「川渡し神事」を在地では[[西大寺 (岡山市)|西大寺]]（[[岡山県]]）の裸行事（会陽）、[[防府天満宮]]（[[山口県]]）の裸坊祭に並ぶ日本三大裸祭りとしている。また、この行事の間、川の中に灯される長さ約16メートル、重約さ5[[トン]]の[[松明|大松明]]もよく知られている。 File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| 秋季大祭の川渡し神事 === ホーランエンヤ === {{Main|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)}} [[江戸時代]]中期に島原藩や[[大坂]]の[[蔵屋敷]]へ[[年貢米]]を回送する廻送船の航海安全を祈願する目的で、[[元旦]]の神事として創められたという漕船行事。現在は1月上旬の日曜日に行われている。 == 社殿 == 現代の社殿は天保4年に造営された[[流造|三間社流造]]の[[檜皮葺]]。正面に[[千鳥破風]]を構え、その下に向[[唐破風]]の[[縋破風]]を飾る。 == 境内 == 粟嶋社、宮地嶽神社、稲荷神社、琴平宮、祇園社の境内社がある。 神門前の小川に架かる石造橋は、[[万延]]元年（[[1860年]]）の建造で、石造[[勾欄]]付き幅4.07メートル長さ3メートル、[[径間]]3.08メートルの[[反橋]]。15から20個の石を組んで拱（[[アーチ]]）の基礎を築く[[アーチ構造]]で、小口（外面部）に16個の縁取りの[[輪石|輪石（わいし）]]を添えて神橋に相応しい丁寧な仕上げを見せる『大分県の文化財』、大分県教育委員会、平成3年。。[[昭和]]58年（[[1983年]]）に大分県の[[有形文化財]]に指定された。 == 文化財 == 括弧内は種別と指定年月日 ; 大分県指定 * 石造橋（[[有形文化財]]、1983年（昭和58年）4月12日） * 本殿、申殿、唐門、西門（有形文化財、2017年（平成29年）3月7日）[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16世紀 府内のメダイなど 県指定文化財7件] 大分合同新聞、2017年2月22日 * ホーランエンヤ（[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、1978年（昭和53年）3月31日） == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 高橋秀雄・染矢多喜男編『祭礼行事・大分県』桜楓社、1993年、ISBN 4-273-02538-8 == 交通 == * 鉄道・バス：[[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[日豊本線]][[宇佐駅]]からバスで約10分、市役所前停留所下車。 * 自動車：[[宇佐別府道路]][[宇佐インターチェンジ|宇佐IC]]より、[[国道213号]]で[[豊後高田市|豊後高田]]方面へ約30分。 == 外部リンク == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ 若宮八幡秋季大祭 裸祭り]（豊後大たいまつ実行委員会） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかみやはちまんしんしや ふんこたかたし}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:大分県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:豊後高田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後高田市の歴史]] [[Category:仁徳天皇]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] to [[Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Bungotakada)]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称 = 若宮八幡神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|サムネイル]] |所在地 = 大分県豊後高田市高田671 |位置 = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = 大鷦鷯尊 |社格 = 旧県社 |創建 = 伝仁寿2年（852年） |本殿 = 三間社流造檜皮葺 |別名 = 若宮様 |札所等 = |例祭 = 陰暦10月15日に近い金曜日から日曜日 |神事 = 神幸祭・川渡し神事（例祭日に同じ）
ホーランエンヤ（1月上旬の日曜日） |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=若宮八幡神社|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }} '''若宮八幡神社'''（わかみやはちまんじんじゃ）は[[大分県]][[豊後高田市]]に鎮座する[[神社]]。[[旧社格]]は[[県社]]。通称'''若宮様'''（わかみやさま）。 正月に行われる[[ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|ホーランエンヤ]]と11月（陰暦10月）に行われる秋季大祭の'''川渡し神事'''（裸祭り）の2つの伝統行事で有名。また、[[紫陽花]]の名所としても有名である。 == 祭神 == '''大鷦鷯尊'''（おほさざきのみこと。[[仁徳天皇]]）を主祭神に、[[天照大御神]]、[[玉祖命]]、'''品陀別命'''（ほむたわけのみこと、[[応神天皇]]）、[[隼総別皇子]]、[[大葉枝皇子]]、[[小葉枝皇子]]、[[雌鳥皇女]]、[[多岐都比売命]]、[[多紀理毘売命]]、[[市寸嶋比売命]]、[[天忍穂耳尊|天之忍穗耳命]]、[[天之菩卑能命]]、[[天津日子根命]]、[[活津日子根命]]、[[熊野久須毘命|熊野久須比命]]（多岐都比売命以下は所謂[[五男三女神]]）を配祀する。 == 由緒沿革 == [[国東半島]]の6郷のうちの[[来縄郷|来縄（くなわ）郷]]他は[[武蔵郷|武蔵（むさし）]]、[[国東郷|国東]]、[[田染郷|田染（たしぶ）]]、[[安岐郷|安岐（あき）]]、[[伊美郷|伊美（いみ）]]の5郷。における[[宇佐神宮]]の[[別宮]]で、[[天長]]元年（[[824年]]）に[[豊前国]][[宇佐郡]]の住人、[[大神蘊麻呂]]の母の酒井門主女に[[神託]]が下り、それを受けて[[仁寿]]2年（[[852年]]）の12月に創祀されたと伝えられる。なお、天照大神、玉祖神、品陀別命の3柱は、[[養老]]2年（[[718年]]）に豊後高田市内の字御玉に出現したという玉を神体として祀られていたという。 [[元亀]]2年（[[1571年]]）と[[天正]]7年（[[1579年]]）の二度にわたり[[キリシタン大名]]である[[大友義鎮|大友宗麟]]らによって焼き討ちされ、天正度においては[[大友氏]]に背いた[[田原親貫]]に味方したとして[[大友義統]]から[[社領]]を没収されたが、[[文禄]]3年（[[1594年]]）に[[高田城 (豊後国)|高田城]]城主に封じられた[[竹中重利]]により文禄年中に再興された。その後高田を領した[[島原藩]]藩主[[松平忠房 (島原藩主)|松平忠房]]により[[寛文]]9年（[[1669年]]）に高田の宗祠と定められ、以来歴代藩主の尊崇を受け、[[天保]]4年（[[1833年]]）には藩主[[松平忠侯]]によって社殿が造替された。 [[明治]]4年（[[1871年]]）に[[郷社]]に列し、同23年6月に県社に昇格した。なお、[[昭和]]30年（[[1955年]]）に[[桂川 (大分県)|桂川]]の改修のために約80[[メートル]]東方に移転している。 == 祭祀 == === 秋季大祭 === 陰暦10月中旬に豊作を感謝する秋季大祭（例祭）が斎行される。例祭にともない[[神幸祭]]と[[神輿]]が桂川を渡る「川渡し神事」が行われ、これは[[永保]]4年（[[1084年]]）に荒行のひとつとして始められたと伝えられるが、神輿の舁き手の姿から「裸祭り」とも呼ばれている。かつては陰暦10月14日から16日にかけて行われていたが、現在は陰暦10月15日に近い金曜日から日曜日にかけて斎行されている。 祭りの一週間前に当神社から神幸先の宮町二区の[[お旅所]]（下宮）までの要所要所に注連を立て、金曜日午後から神輿が当神社を進発する（お下り）。神輿の舁き手には陸組と川組とがあるが、まず頭に[[鉢巻]]を締めた白装束の男子数十人からなる陸組に担がれて豊後高田市の市役所を練った後、夕刻に御玉橋の右岸袂に建つ大鳥居で川組に交代、川組は頭に鉢巻を下半身に[[褌]]と[[腹帯 (曖昧さ回避)|腹帯]]を締めて白衣を着した[[草鞋]]履きの男子数十人からなり、神輿を担いで桂川に入り、浮き沈みしながらも掛け声を上げ、数隻のお供船に導かれつつ川を渡る。対岸に着くと再度陸組に代わり、町を練って下宮に入る。その後神輿は下宮に駐輦し、日曜日午後に下宮を進発（お上り）、再び桂川を渡る「川渡し神事」を経て当神社に還幸する染矢多喜男「大分の祭礼行事・解説 裸祭り」（『祭礼行事・大分県』所収）。。 この「川渡し神事」を在地では[[西大寺 (岡山市)|西大寺]]（[[岡山県]]）の裸行事（会陽）、[[防府天満宮]]（[[山口県]]）の裸坊祭に並ぶ日本三大裸祭りとしている。また、この行事の間、川の中に灯される長さ約16メートル、重約さ5[[トン]]の[[松明|大松明]]もよく知られている。 File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| 秋季大祭の川渡し神事 === ホーランエンヤ === {{Main|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)}} [[江戸時代]]中期に島原藩や[[大坂]]の[[蔵屋敷]]へ[[年貢米]]を回送する廻送船の航海安全を祈願する目的で、[[元旦]]の神事として創められたという漕船行事。現在は1月上旬の日曜日に行われている。 == 社殿 == 現代の社殿は天保4年に造営された[[流造|三間社流造]]の[[檜皮葺]]。正面に[[千鳥破風]]を構え、その下に向[[唐破風]]の[[縋破風]]を飾る。 == 境内 == 粟嶋社、宮地嶽神社、稲荷神社、琴平宮、祇園社の境内社がある。 神門前の小川に架かる石造橋は、[[万延]]元年（[[1860年]]）の建造で、石造[[勾欄]]付き幅4.07メートル長さ3メートル、[[径間]]3.08メートルの[[反橋]]。15から20個の石を組んで拱（[[アーチ]]）の基礎を築く[[アーチ構造]]で、小口（外面部）に16個の縁取りの[[輪石|輪石（わいし）]]を添えて神橋に相応しい丁寧な仕上げを見せる『大分県の文化財』、大分県教育委員会、平成3年。。[[昭和]]58年（[[1983年]]）に大分県の[[有形文化財]]に指定された。 == 文化財 == 括弧内は種別と指定年月日 ; 大分県指定 * 石造橋（[[有形文化財]]、1983年（昭和58年）4月12日） * 本殿、申殿、唐門、西門（有形文化財、2017年（平成29年）3月7日）[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16世紀 府内のメダイなど 県指定文化財7件] 大分合同新聞、2017年2月22日 * ホーランエンヤ（[[選択無形民俗文化財]]、1978年（昭和53年）3月31日） == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 高橋秀雄・染矢多喜男編『祭礼行事・大分県』桜楓社、1993年、ISBN 4-273-02538-8 == 交通 == * 鉄道・バス：[[九州旅客鉄道|JR九州]][[日豊本線]][[宇佐駅]]からバスで約10分、市役所前停留所下車。 * 自動車：[[宇佐別府道路]][[宇佐インターチェンジ|宇佐IC]]より、[[国道213号]]で[[豊後高田市|豊後高田]]方面へ約30分。 == 外部リンク == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ 若宮八幡秋季大祭 裸祭り]（豊後大たいまつ実行委員会） {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:わかみやはちまんしんしや ふんこたかたし}} [[Category:大分県の神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:大分県の旧県社]] [[Category:大分県指定有形文化財]] [[Category:豊後高田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後高田市の歴史]] [[Category:仁徳天皇]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Bungotakada|ja|豊後高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|ja|防府天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|粟嶋社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|ja|宇佐駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Bungotakada|ja|豊後高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|ja|防府天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine|ja|粟嶋社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|ja|宇佐駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)
{{shinto-stub}}

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Bungotakada|ja|豊後高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.

It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.

It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|ja|防府天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Yamaguchi.

The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|qq=}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|qq=}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|qq=}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|ja|防府天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfu Tenmangū|qq=}} in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nagare-zukuri|qq=}} design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|ja|宇佐駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Main Line|qq=}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|qq=}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|qq=}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Bungotakada|ja|豊後高田市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bungotakada|qq=}}, Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|qq=}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|ja|玉祖命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|qq=}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|ja|宇佐神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Jingū|qq=}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|qq=}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|qq=}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikoshi|qq=}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|ja|防府天満宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōfu Tenmangū|qq=}} in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay {{ill|nagare-zukuri|ja|流造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/nagare-zukuri|qq=}} design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|ja|宇佐駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|ja|日豊本線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nippō Main Line|qq=}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|qq=}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)
{{shinto-stub}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Bungotakada, Ōita|Bungotakada]], Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|qq=}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * [[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|qq=}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]]. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|qq=}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|qq=}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and [[:en:Hōfu Tenmangū|Hōfu Tenmangū]] in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]] design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|qq=}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|qq=}} Usa IC via Route 213.

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Bungotakada, Ōita|Bungotakada]], Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|qq=}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * [[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|qq=}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]]. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|qq=}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|qq=}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and [[:en:Hōfu Tenmangū|Hōfu Tenmangū]] in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]] design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|qq=}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|qq=}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

[[sn:Wakamiya Hachiman-gū (Bungotakada)]]
[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] [[sn:akamiya Hachiman-gū (Bungotakada)]] [[Category:仁徳天皇]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Oita Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:豊後高田市の建築物]] [[Category:豊後高田市の歴史]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Bungotakada, Ōita|Bungotakada]], Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|qq=}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} * [[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|qq=}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime|qq=}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|qq=}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|qq=}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]]. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|qq=}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|qq=}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|qq=}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and [[:en:Hōfu Tenmangū|Hōfu Tenmangū]] in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>

A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.

== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|qq=}} Usa IC via Route 213.

It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and [[:en:Hōfu Tenmangū|Hōfu Tenmangū]] in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]] design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)
