'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Bungotakada, Ōita|Bungotakada]], Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]]. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|1=Takenaka Shigetoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598745}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and [[:en:Hōfu Tenmangū|Hōfu Tenmangū]] in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]] design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)
{{shinto-stub}}

== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616260]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Bungotakada, Ōita|Bungotakada]], Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]]. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|1=Takenaka Shigetoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598745}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and [[:en:Hōfu Tenmangū|Hōfu Tenmangū]] in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]] design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]]. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|1=Takenaka Shigetoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598745}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and [[:en:Hōfu Tenmangū|Hōfu Tenmangū]] in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]] design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Bungotakada, Ōita|Bungotakada]], Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.

[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]]
[[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Oita Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Buildings in Bungotakada City]] [[Category:History of Bungotakada City]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11616260}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616260]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Bungotakada, Ōita|Bungotakada]], Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]]. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|1=Takenaka Shigetoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598745}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and [[:en:Hōfu Tenmangū|Hōfu Tenmangū]] in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]] design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}

During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|1=Takenaka Shigetoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598745}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and [[:en:Hōfu Tenmangū|Hōfu Tenmangū]] in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]] design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Bungotakada, Ōita|Bungotakada]], Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616260]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in [[:en:Bungotakada, Ōita|Bungotakada]], Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of [[:en:Usa Jingū|Usa Jingū]]. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|1=Takenaka Shigetoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598745}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and [[:en:Hōfu Tenmangū|Hōfu Tenmangū]] in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]] design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * [[:en:Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]] * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}

During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|1=Takenaka Shigetoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598745}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the [[:en:Mikoshi|Mikoshi]]. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and [[:en:Hōfu Tenmangū|Hōfu Tenmangū]] in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay [[:en:Nagare-zukuri|nagare-zukuri]] design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from [[:en:Usa Station|Usa Station]] on the [[:en:Nippō Main Line|Nippō Main Line]]. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)
{{shinto-stub}}

'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Bungotakada, Ōita|en|Bungotakada, Ōita|WD=Q857281|lt=Bungotakada}}, Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|en|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|WD=Q11572729|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|1=Takenaka Shigetoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598745}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|en|Hōfu Tenmangū|WD=Q704898|lt=Hōfu Tenmangū}} in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.

== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=nagare-zukuri}} design with a cypress bark roof.

== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|en|Usa Station|WD=Q5360282|lt=Usa Station}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

{{wikidata link|Q11616260}} [[Category:Buildings in Bungotakada City]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Emperor Nintoku]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Oita Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:History of Bungotakada City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Oita Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11616260]] Wikidata name: Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:19, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in {{ill|Bungotakada, Ōita|en|Bungotakada, Ōita|WD=Q857281|lt=Bungotakada}}, Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|en|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|WD=Q11572729|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|1=Takenaka Shigetoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598745}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|en|Hōfu Tenmangū|WD=Q704898|lt=Hōfu Tenmangū}} in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=nagare-zukuri}} design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|en|Usa Station|WD=Q5360282|lt=Usa Station}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|en|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|WD=Q11572729|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Ōjin|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|1=Takenaka Shigetoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598745}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|en|Hōfu Tenmangū|WD=Q704898|lt=Hōfu Tenmangū}} in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=nagare-zukuri}} design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|en|Usa Station|WD=Q5360282|lt=Usa Station}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|en|Usa Station|WD=Q5360282|lt=Usa Station}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa Beppu road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:若宮八幡神社 (豊後高田市)]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine Bungotakada.jpg|thumb]] |location = Bungotakada, Ōita Prefecture, Takada 671 |Position = {{Coord|33|33|17.7|N|131|26|46.7|E|region:JP-44_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |deity = Ōsazaki no Mikoto (Emperor Nintoku) |type = Former Prefectural Shrine |Founding = Traditional date: 852 (Ninju 2) |Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri, Cypress Bark Roof |Other names = Wakamiya-sama |Festival Dates = Closest Friday-Sunday to the lunar October 15 |Rituals = Sacred Procession and River Crossing Ceremony (on festival dates), Horan Enya (early January) |Map = {{location map|Japan Oita Prefecture|label=Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine|float=center|relief=1|width=256}} }}
'''Wakamiya Hachiman Shrine''' (若宮八幡神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Bungotakada, Ōita|en|Bungotakada, Ōita|WD=Q857281|lt=Bungotakada|1=Bungotakada, Ōita}}, Ōita Prefecture. It holds the Rank of a former {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} and is commonly referred to as "Wakamiya-sama" (若宮様).
The shrine is renowned for its two traditional festivals: the {{ill|Horan Enya (Ōita)|ja|ホーランエンヤ (大分県)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horan Enya (Ōita)|qq=}} held in early January, and the autumn festival's {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (also known as the Naked Festival) held in November. The site is also famed for its hydrangeas.
== Deities == The principal deity is '''Ōsazaki no Mikoto''' (Emperor Nintoku). The following deities are also enshrined: * {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天照大神}} * {{ill|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|en|Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|WD=Q11572729|lt=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto|1=Tamanooya-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Hachiman|ja|品陀別命|1=Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Hondawake no Mikoto|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachiman|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 応神天皇|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q317997}} (Emperor Ōjin) * {{ill|Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja|隼総別皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hayasumi Wakatake Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Ōwae Mikoto|ja|大葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōwae Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja|小葉枝皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kowaeshi Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Menotori no Miko|ja|雌鳥皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menotori no Miko|qq=}} * {{ill|Tagitsuhime|ja|多岐都比売命|1=Tagitsuhime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tagitsuhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タギツヒメ}} * {{ill|Ichikishima-hime|ja|市寸嶋比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ichikishima-hime||1=Ichikishima-hime|2=fr|3=Ichikishimahime|4=ja|5=イチキシマヒメ|6=zh|7=市寸島比賣命|8=ru|9=Итикишима-химэ|10=de|11=Ichikishima-hime}} * {{ill|Takamimusubi|ja|高皇産霊尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamimusubi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タカミムスビ}} * {{ill|Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja|五男三女神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five Male and Three Female Deities|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アマテラスとスサノオの誓約}}
== History == According to tradition, the shrine was established in 852 (Ninju 2) as a branch of {{ill|Usa Jingū|en|Usa Jingū|WD=Q715632|lt=Usa Jingū|1=Usa Jingū}}. It is said that in 824 (Tenchō 1), a divine oracle was received by a resident of Usa, leading to the shrine's founding.
During the late 16th century, the shrine suffered attacks during the rule of Christian daimyō {{ill|Ōtomo Sōrin|ja|大友宗麟|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtomo Sōrin|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大友義鎮|1=Ōtomo Sōrin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1185032}}, but it was restored in the early 17th century by {{ill|Takenaka Shigetoshi|ja|竹中重利|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takenaka Shigetoshi|1=Q11598745|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11598745}}. It became the principal shrine of Takada and received patronage from successive feudal lords.
In the Meiji era, it was designated as a Prefectural Shrine, and in 1955, it was relocated approximately 80 meters east due to river reconstruction.
== Festivals == === Autumn Grand Festival === The Autumn Grand Festival is held to express gratitude for the harvest. It includes the famous {{ill|River Crossing Ceremony|ja|川渡し神事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/River Crossing Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, also known as the Naked Festival. This ceremony, first recorded in 1084 (Eihō 4), features shrine bearers crossing the Katsura River with the {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=Mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}}. It is considered one of Japan's three major naked festivals, alongside the events at {{ill|Saidaiji Temple|ja|西大寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saidaiji Temple|qq=}} in Okayama and {{ill|Hōfu Tenmangū|en|Hōfu Tenmangū|WD=Q704898|lt=Hōfu Tenmangū|1=Hōfu Tenmangū}} in Yamaguchi.
During the procession, the mikoshi is carried by groups alternating between land bearers and river bearers. The latter wade through the Katsura River wearing white loincloths and chanting loudly. A large ceremonial torch, measuring 16 meters and weighing 5 tons, is also prominently displayed.
<gallery> File:Wakamiya Hachiman Naked Festival.jpg| River Crossing Ceremony at the Autumn Grand Festival </gallery>
=== Horan Enya === {{Main|Horan Enya (Ōita)}} This ceremonial boat procession was initiated in the Edo period to pray for safe sea transport. It is now held annually in early January.
== Architecture == The current main hall, constructed in 1833 (Tenpō 4), features a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|WD=Q210684|lt=nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} design with a cypress bark roof.
== Grounds == The shrine grounds house several auxiliary shrines, including: * {{ill|Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|ja|粟嶋社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awashima Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Miyajidake Shrine * Inari Shrine * Kotohira Shrine * Gion Shrine
A stone arched bridge, constructed in 1860 (Man'en 1), spans a stream near the shrine gate. It is a meticulously crafted structure designated as an Ōita Prefectural Cultural Property.
== Cultural Properties == <small>Dates in parentheses indicate designation dates.</small> ; Ōita Prefectural Designation * Stone Bridge (Tangible Cultural Property, 1983) * Main Hall, Worship Hall, Karamon Gate, and Western Gate (Tangible Cultural Property, 2017)<ref>[http://www.oita-press.co.jp/1010000000/2017/02/22/JD0055491938 16th Century Medals and Other Cultural Properties Recognized in Ōita]</ref> * Horan Enya (Selected Intangible Folk Cultural Property, 1978)
== Access == * Train/Bus: Approximately 10 minutes by bus from {{ill|Usa Station|en|Usa Station|WD=Q5360282|lt=Usa Station|1=Usa Station}} on the {{ill|Nippō Main Line|en|Nippō Main Line|WD=Q1052288|lt=Nippō Main Line|1=Nippō Main Line}}. * Car: About 30 minutes from {{ill|Usa-Beppu Road|ja|宇佐別府道路|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Usa-Beppu Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Usa-Beppu Road|WD=Q2743567}} Usa IC via Route 213.
== External Links == * [http://hadaka-matsuri.com/ Wakamiya Hachiman Autumn Grand Festival - Naked Festival] (Official Site)
{{shinto-stub}}
